1.arabic: بِسْمِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنِ ٱلرَّحِيمِ ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَنزَلَ عَلَىٰ عَبْدِهِ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ وَلَمْ يَجْعَل لَّهُۥ عِوَجَا ۜ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: ALL PRAISE BE to God who has revealed to His votary the Book which is free of all obliquity,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: All praise is to Allah Who sent down the Book upon His bondman, and has not kept any deviation in it.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Praise belongs to God who has sent down upon His servant the Book and has not assigned unto it any crookedness;

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: All praise unto Allah who hath sent down unto His bondman the Book, and hath not placed therein any crookedness.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: All the praises and thanks be to Allah, Who has sent down to His slave (Muhammad SAW) the Book (the Quran), and has not placed therein any crookedness.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Praise be to God, who revealed the Book to His servant, and allowed in it no distortion.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Praise be to Allah Who has revealed to His servant the Book devoid of all crookedness;

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: All praise is due to Allah, Who has sent down to His servant the Book, and has not placed therein any crookedness.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Praise be to Allah Who hath revealed the Scripture unto His slave, and hath not placed therein any crookedness,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: All praise belongs to Allah, who has sent down the Book to His servant and did not let any crookedness be in it,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Praise belongs to Allah who has sent down the Book to His worshiper (Prophet Muhammad) and has not made any crookedness in it,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [All] praise is [due] to Allah, who has sent down upon His Servant the Book and has not made therein any deviance.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Praise be to God. He has sent the Book to His servant and has made it a flawless guide (for human beings)

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: (All) praise is due to Allah, Who revealed the Book to His servant and did not make in it any crookedness.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Praise be to God who has sent down to His servant -- the Book, which is free from any ambiguity

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Praise be to Allah, Who hath sent to His Servant the Book, and hath allowed therein no Crookedness:

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Alhamdu lillahi allathee anzala AAala AAabdihi alkitaba walam yajAAal lahu AAiwajan

2.arabic: قَيِّمًا لِّيُنذِرَ بَأْسًا شَدِيدًا مِّن لَّدُنْهُ وَيُبَشِّرَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ ٱلَّذِينَ يَعْمَلُونَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ أَنَّ لَهُمْ أَجْرًا حَسَنًا

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Immutable, so that it may warn of a severe punishment from Him; and give happy tidings to those who believe and do the right that there is a better reward (of Paradise) for them,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: A just Book, to warn of Allah’s severe punishment, and to give glad tidings to the believers who do good deeds, that for them is an excellent reward.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: right, to warn of great violence from Him, and to give good tidings unto the believers, who do righteous deeds, that theirs shall be a goodly wage

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Straightforward, that it may warn of a severe violence from before Him, and bring glad tidings to the believers who work righteous works that theirs shall be a goodly hire.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: (He has made it) Straight to give warning (to the disbelievers) of a severe punishment from Him, and to give glad tidings to the believers (in the Oneness of Allah Islamic Monotheism), who work righteous deeds, that they shall have a fair reward (i.e. Paradise).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Valuable—to warn of severe punishment from Himself; and to deliver good news to the believers who do righteous deeds, that they will have an excellent reward.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: an unerringly Straight Book, meant to warn of a stern punishment from Allah, and to proclaim, to those who believe and work righteous deeds, the tiding that theirs shall be a good reward

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: (He has made it) straight to give warning of a severe punishment from Him, and to give good news to the believers, who do righteous deeds, that they shall have a fair reward.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: (But hath made it) straight, to give warning of stern punishment from Him, and to bring unto the believers who do good works the news that theirs will be a fair reward,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: [a Book] upright, to warn of a severe punishment from Him, and to give good news to the faithful who do righteous deeds, that there shall be for them a good reward,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: unswerving. To warn of great violence from Him, and to give good tidings to the believers who do good deeds that theirs shall be a goodly wage

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [He has made it] straight, to warn of severe punishment from Him and to give good tidings to the believers who do righteous deeds that they will have a good reward

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: so that he could warn them of His stern retribution, give the glad news of the best and everlasting reward to the righteously striving believers,

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Rightly directing, that he might give warning of severe punishment from Him and give good news to the believers who do good that they shall have a goodly reward,

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: and which rightly directs, to give warning of stern punishment from Him, and to proclaim to the believers who do righteous deeds that they shall have an excellent recompense,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: (He hath made it) Straight (and Clear) in order that He may warn (the godless) of a terrible Punishment from Him, and that He may give Glad Tidings to the Believers who work righteous deeds, that they shall have a goodly Reward,

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Qayyiman liyunthira basan shadeedan min ladunhu wayubashshira almumineena allatheena yaAAmaloona alssalihati anna lahum ajran hasanan

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Where they will abide for ever;

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: In which they will abide forever.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: therein to abide for ever,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: They will abide therein for ever.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: They shall abide therein forever.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: In which they will abide forever.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: wherein they shall abide for ever;

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: They shall abide therein forever.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Wherein they will abide for ever;

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: to abide in it forever,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: and they will live for ever therein.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: In which they will remain forever

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: wherein they shall remain forever,

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Staying in it for ever;

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: wherein they will remain [in a state of bliss] forever.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Wherein they shall remain for ever:

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Makitheena feehi abadan

4.arabic: وَيُنذِرَ ٱلَّذِينَ قَالُوا۟ ٱتَّخَذَ ٱللَّهُ وَلَدًا

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And that it may warn those who say: "God has begotten a son."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And to warn those who say “Allah has chosen a child.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: and to warn those who say, 'God has taken to Himself a son';

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And that it may warn those who say: God hath taken a son.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And to warn those (Jews, Christians, and pagans) who say, "Allah has begotten a son (or offspring or children)."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And to warn those who say, “God has begotten a son.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: and also to warn those who say: "Allah has taken to Himself a son,"

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And to warn those who say, "Allah has begotten a child."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And to warn those who say: Allah hath chosen a son,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: and to warn those who say, ‘Allah has taken a son.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: And it (the Koran) warns those who say: 'Allah has taken a son'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And to warn those who say, "Allah has taken a son."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: and admonish those who say that God has begotten a son.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And warn those who say: Allah has taken a son.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: And to warn those who say, "God has taken to Himself a son."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Further, that He may warn those (also) who say, "Allah hath begotten a son":

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wayunthira allatheena qaloo ittakhatha Allahu waladan

5.arabic: مَّا لَهُم بِهِۦ مِنْ عِلْمٍ وَلَا لِـَٔابَآئِهِمْ ۚ كَبُرَتْ كَلِمَةً تَخْرُجُ مِنْ أَفْوَٰهِهِمْ ۚ إِن يَقُولُونَ إِلَّا كَذِبًا

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: They have no knowledge of this, as their fathers did not have. How terrible are the words they utter! They speak nothing but lies.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: They do not have any knowledge of it – nor did their forefathers; profound is the word that comes out of their mouths; they only speak a lie.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: they have no knowledge of it, they nor their fathers; a monstrous word it is, issuing out of their mouths; they say nothing but a lie.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: No knowledge thereof have they, nor had their fathers. Odious is the word that cometh out of their mouths; they say not but a lie.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: No knowledge have they of such a thing, nor had their fathers. Mighty is the word that comes out of their mouths [i.e. He begot (took) sons and daughters]. They utter nothing but a lie.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: They have no knowledge of this, nor did their forefathers. Grave is the word that comes out of their mouths. They say nothing but a lie.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: a thing about which they have no knowledge, neither they nor their ancestors. Dreadful is the word that comes out of their mouths. What they utter is merely a lie.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: No knowledge have they of such a thing, nor had their fathers. Mighty is the word that comes out of their mouths. They utter nothing but a lie.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: (A thing) whereof they have no knowledge, nor (had) their fathers, Dreadful is the word that cometh out of their mouths. They speak naught but a lie.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They do not have any knowledge of that, nor did their fathers. Monstrous is the utterance that comes out of their mouths, and they say nothing but a lie.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: . Surely, of this they have no knowledge, neither they nor their fathers; it is a monstrous word that comes from their mouths, they say nothing but a lie.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: They have no knowledge of it, nor had their fathers. Grave is the word that comes out of their mouths; they speak not except a lie.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Neither they nor their fathers had any knowledge of such utterance (that God has begotten a son). Whatever they say about (this matter) is vicious blasphemy and plain lies.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: They have no knowledge of it, nor had their fathers; a grievous word it is that comes out of their mouths; they speak nothing but a lie.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: They have no knowledge of this, nor did their forefathers have any either. What they say is monstrous: they are merely uttering falsehoods!

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: No knowledge have they of such a thing, nor had their fathers. It is a grievous thing that issues from their mouths as a saying what they say is nothing but falsehood!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Ma lahum bihi min AAilmin wala liabaihim kaburat kalimatan takhruju min afwahihim in yaqooloona illa kathiban

6.arabic: فَلَعَلَّكَ بَـٰخِعٌ نَّفْسَكَ عَلَىٰٓ ءَاثَـٰرِهِمْ إِن لَّمْ يُؤْمِنُوا۟ بِهَـٰذَا ٱلْحَدِيثِ أَسَفًا

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Will you kill yourself for grief of them if they do not believe in this presentation?

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Possibly you may risk your life by grieving (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him) for them if they do not believe in this narration.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Yet perchance, if they believe not in this tiding, thou wilt consume thyself, following after them, of grief.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Haply thou art going to kill thyself over their footsteps, if they believe not in this discourse, out of sorrow.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Perhaps, you, would kill yourself (O Muhammad SAW) in grief, over their footsteps (for their turning away from you), because they believe not in this narration (the Quran).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Perhaps you may destroy yourself with grief, chasing after them, if they do not believe in this information.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: (O Muhammad), if they do not believe in this Message, you will perhaps torment yourself to death with grief, sorrowing over them.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Perhaps, you would kill yourself in grief, over their footsteps, because they believe not in this narration.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Yet it may be, if they believe not in this statement, that thou (Muhammad) wilt torment thy soul with grief over their footsteps.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: You are liable to imperil your life out of grief for their sake, if they should not believe this discourse.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Yet perchance, if they do not believe in this tiding, you will consume yourself with grief and follow after them.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Then perhaps you would kill yourself through grief over them, [O Muhammad], if they do not believe in this message, [and] out of sorrow.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Perhaps you will destroy yourself out of grief because they disbelieve this Book.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Then maybe you will kill yourself with grief, sorrowing after them, if they do not believe in this announcement.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Perhaps you may destroy yourself with grief if they do not believe in this message.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Thou wouldst only, perchance, fret thyself to death, following after them, in grief, if they believe not in this Message.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: FalaAAallaka bakhiAAun nafsaka AAala atharihim in lam yuminoo bihatha alhadeethi asafan

7.arabic: إِنَّا جَعَلْنَا مَا عَلَى ٱلْأَرْضِ زِينَةً لَّهَا لِنَبْلُوَهُمْ أَيُّهُمْ أَحْسَنُ عَمَلًا

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: We have made whatever exists on the earth its adornment to test and try them (and) know who acts better;

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: We have indeed placed all that is on the earth as its adornment in order that We may test them, who among them has the best deeds.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: We have appointed all that is on the earth for an adornment for it, and that We may try which of them is fairest in works;

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Verily We have made whatsoever is on the earth as an adornment thereof, that We may prove them - which of them is best in work.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Verily! We have made that which is on earth as an adornment for it, in order that We may test them (mankind) as to which of them are best in deeds. [i.e. those who do good deeds in the most perfect manner, that means to do them (deeds) totally for Allah's sake and in accordance to the legal ways of the Prophet SAW].

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: We made what is upon the earth an ornament for it, to test them as to which of them is best in conduct.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Surely We have made all that is on the earth an embellishment for it in order to test people as to who of them is better in conduct.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Verily, we have made that which is on earth an adornment for it, in order that We may test which of them are best in deeds.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Lo! We have placed all that is on the earth as an ornament thereof that We may try them: which of them is best in conduct.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Indeed We have made whatever is on the earth an adornment for it that We may test them [to see] which of them is best in conduct.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: We have appointed all that is on the earth an adornment for it, in order that We try which of them is finest in works.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Indeed, We have made that which is on the earth adornment for it that We may test them [as to] which of them is best in deed.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We have caused earthly things to seem attractive so that We can see who will excel in good deeds.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Surely We have made whatever is on the earth an embellishment for it, so that We may try them (as to) which of them is best in works.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: We have adorned the earth with attractive things, so that We may test mankind as to which one is best in conduct,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: That which is on earth we have made but as a glittering show for the earth, in order that We may test them - as to which of them are best in conduct.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Inna jaAAalna ma AAala alardi zeenatan laha linabluwahum ayyuhum ahsanu AAamalan

8.arabic: وَإِنَّا لَجَـٰعِلُونَ مَا عَلَيْهَا صَعِيدًا جُرُزًا

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: For We shall certainly turn it to barren dust.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And indeed We shall one day make all that is on it a barren plain.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: and We shall surely make all that is on it barren dust.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And verily We are going to make whatsoever is thereon a soil bare.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And verily! We shall make all that is on it (the earth) a bare dry soil (without any vegetation or trees, etc.).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And We will turn what is on it into barren waste.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: In the ultimate, We shall reduce all that is on the earth to a barren plain.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And verily, We shall make all that is on it bare, dry soil.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And lo! We shall make all that is thereon a barren mound.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: And indeed We will turn whatever is on it into a barren plain.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: We will surely reduce all that is on it to barren dust.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And indeed, We will make that which is upon it [into] a barren ground.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Let it be known that We will turn all things on earth into dust.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And most surely We will make what is on it bare ground without herbage.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: but We shall reduce all this to barren waste.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Verily what is on earth we shall make but as dust and dry soil (without growth or herbage).

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wainna lajaAAiloona ma AAalayha saAAeedan juruzan

9.arabic: أَمْ حَسِبْتَ أَنَّ أَصْحَـٰبَ ٱلْكَهْفِ وَٱلرَّقِيمِ كَانُوا۟ مِنْ ءَايَـٰتِنَا عَجَبًا

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Do you think the men of the cave and Ar-Raqim were so strange among Our signs?

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Did you know that the People of the Cave and People close to the Woods, were Our exceptional signs?

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Or dost thou think the Men of the Cave and Er-Rakeem were among Our signs a wonder?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Deemest thou that the people of the cave and the inscription were of Our signs a wonder?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Do you think that the people of the Cave and the Inscription (the news or the names of the people of the Cave) were a wonder among Our Signs?

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Did you know that the People of the Cave and the Inscription were of Our wondrous signs?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Do you think that the people of the Cave and the Inscription were one of Our wondrous signs?

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Do you think that the people of Al-Kahf and Ar-Raqim were a wonder among Our signs

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Or deemest thou that the People of the Cave and the Inscription are a wonder among Our portents?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Do you suppose that the Companions of the Cave and the Inscription were among Our wonderful signs?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Or, do you think the companions of the Cave and the tomb stone were a wonder among Our signs?

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Or have you thought that the companions of the cave and the inscription were, among Our signs, a wonder?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Do you not think that the story of the Companions of the Cave and the Inscription was one of Our marvelous miracles?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Or, do you think that the Fellows of the Cave and the Inscription were of Our wonderful signs?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Do you think that the Men of the Cave and the Inscription were one of Our wondrous signs?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Or dost thou reflect that the Companions of the Cave and of the Inscription were wonders among Our Sign?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Am hasibta anna ashaba alkahfi waalrraqeemi kanoo min ayatina AAajaban

10.arabic: إِذْ أَوَى ٱلْفِتْيَةُ إِلَى ٱلْكَهْفِ فَقَالُوا۟ رَبَّنَآ ءَاتِنَا مِن لَّدُنكَ رَحْمَةً وَهَيِّئْ لَنَا مِنْ أَمْرِنَا رَشَدًا

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: When those young men took shelter in the cave, and prayed: "O Lord, grant us Your favour and dispose our affair aright,"

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: When the young men took refuge in the Cave – then said, “Our Lord! Give us mercy from Yourself, and arrange guidance for us in our affair.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: When the youths took refuge in the Cave saying, 'Our lord, give us mercy from Thee, and furnish us with rectitude in our affair.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Recall what time the youths betook themselves to the cave, then said: our Lord! vouchsafe unto us mercy from before Thee, and prepare for us in our affair a right course.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: (Remember) when the young men fled for refuge (from their disbelieving folk) to the Cave, they said: "Our Lord! Bestow on us mercy from Yourself, and facilitate for us our affair in the right way!"

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: When the youths took shelter in the cave, they said, “Our Lord, give us mercy from Yourself, and bless our affair with guidance.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: When those youths sought refuge in the Cave and said: "Our Lord! Grant us mercy from Yourself and provide for us rectitude in our affairs."

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: When the young men fled for refuge to Al-Kahf. They said: "Our Lord! Bestow on us mercy from Yourself, and facilitate for us our affair in the right way!"

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: When the young men fled for refuge to the Cave and said: Our Lord! Give us mercy from Thy presence, and shape for us right conduct in our plight.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: When the youths took refuge in the Cave, they said, ‘Our Lord! Grant us a mercy from Yourself, and help us on to rectitude in our affair.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: When the youths sought refuge in the Cave, they said: 'Lord give us from Your Mercy and furnish us with rectitude in our affair'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [Mention] when the youths retreated to the cave and said, "Our Lord, grant us from Yourself mercy and prepare for us from our affair right guidance."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: When the youth sought refuge in the cave they prayed "Lord, grant us mercy and help us to get out of this trouble in a righteous way".

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: When the youths sought refuge in the cave, they said: Our Lord! grant us mercy from Thee, and provide for us a right course in our affair.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: When the young men sought refuge in the cave, they said, "Our Lord, grant us Your special mercy and give us right guidance in our affair."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Behold, the youths betook themselves to the Cave: they said, "Our Lord! bestow on us Mercy from Thyself, and dispose of our affair for us in the right way!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Ith awa alfityatu ila alkahfi faqaloo rabbana atina min ladunka rahmatan wahayyi lana min amrina rashadan

11.arabic: فَضَرَبْنَا عَلَىٰٓ ءَاذَانِهِمْ فِى ٱلْكَهْفِ سِنِينَ عَدَدًا

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: We sealed off their ears in the cave for a number of years,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: We then thumped upon their ears in the Cave for a number of years. (* Put them to sleep.)

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Then We smote their ears many years in the Cave.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Wherefore We put a covering over their ears in the cave for a number of years.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Therefore We covered up their (sense of) hearing (causing them, to go in deep sleep) in the Cave for a number of years.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Then We sealed their ears in the cave for a number of years.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: We lulled them to sleep in that cave for a number of years

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Therefore, We covered up their hearing in Al-Kahf for a number of years.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Then We sealed up their hearing in the Cave for a number of years.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: So We put them to sleep in the Cave for several years.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: For many years We sealed up their hearing in the Cave,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So We cast [a cover of sleep] over their ears within the cave for a number of years.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We sealed their ears in the cave for a number of years.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: So We prevented them from hearing in the cave for a number of years.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Then We caused them to fall into a deep sleep for many years inside the cave.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Then We draw (a veil) over their ears, for a number of years, in the Cave, (so that they heard not):

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Fadarabna AAala athanihim fee alkahfi sineena AAadadan

12.arabic: ثُمَّ بَعَثْنَـٰهُمْ لِنَعْلَمَ أَىُّ ٱلْحِزْبَيْنِ أَحْصَىٰ لِمَا لَبِثُوٓا۟ أَمَدًا

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Then roused them to ascertain which of the two groups could account for the period they had stayed.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: We then awakened them to see which of the two groups more accurately tells the period they had stayed.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Afterwards. We raised them up again, that We might know which of the two parties would better calculate the while they had tarried.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Thereafter We raised them up that We might know which of the two parties was best at reckoning the time that they had tarried.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Then We raised them up (from their sleep), that We might test which of the two parties was best at calculating the time period that they had tarried.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Then We awakened them to know which of the two groups could better calculate the length of their stay.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: and then roused them so that We might see which of the two parties could best tell the length of their stay.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Then We raised them up, that We might test which of the two parties was best at calculating the time period they tarried.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And afterward We raised them up that We might know which of the two parties would best calculate the time that they had tarried.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Then We aroused them that We might know which of the two groups better reckoned the period they had stayed.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: and thereafter We revived them to find out which of the two parties could best calculate the length of their stay.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Then We awakened them that We might show which of the two factions was most precise in calculating what [extent] they had remained in time.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Then We roused them to find out which of the party had the correct account of the duration of their sleep in the cave.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Then We raised them up that We might know which of the two parties was best able to compute the time for which they remained.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Then We woke them up again so that We might see which of the two groups would better calculate the time they had stayed there.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Then We roused them, in order to test which of the two parties was best at calculating the term of years they had tarried!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Thumma baAAathnahum linaAAlama ayyu alhizbayni ahsa lima labithoo amadan

13.arabic: نَّحْنُ نَقُصُّ عَلَيْكَ نَبَأَهُم بِٱلْحَقِّ ۚ إِنَّهُمْ فِتْيَةٌ ءَامَنُوا۟ بِرَبِّهِمْ وَزِدْنَـٰهُمْ هُدًى

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: We narrate their story to you in all truth. They were a few young men who believed in their Lord; so We gave them greater guidance.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: We shall narrate their account to you accurately; they were young men who believed in their Lord, and We increased the guidance for them.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: We will relate to thee their tidings truly. They were youths who believed in their Lord, and We increased them in guidance.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: We recount unto thee their tidings With truth. Verily they were certain youths who believed in their Lord, and We increased them in guidance.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: We narrate unto you (O Muhammad SAW) their story with truth: Truly! They were young men who believed in their Lord (Allah), and We increased them in guidance.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: We relate to you their story in truth. They were youths who believed in their Lord, and We increased them in guidance.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: We narrate to you their true story. They were a party of young men who had faith in their Lord, and We increased them in guidance

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: We narrate unto you their story with truth: Truly, they were young men who believed in their Lord (Allah), and We increased them in guidance.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: We narrate unto thee their story with truth. Lo! they were young men who believed in their Lord, and We increased them in guidance.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: We relate to you their account in truth. They were indeed youths who had faith in their Lord, and We had enhanced them in guidance,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: In truth We tell to you their news. They were young men who believed in their Lord, and We increased them in guidance.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: It is We who relate to you, [O Muhammad], their story in truth. Indeed, they were youths who believed in their Lord, and We increased them in guidance.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We tell you this story for a genuine purpose. They were young people who believed in their Lord and We gave them further guidance.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: We relate to you their story with the truth; surely they were youths who believed in their Lord and We increased them in guidance.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: We shall tell you their story as it really was. They were young men who believed in their Lord, and on whom We bestowed further guidance.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: We relate to thee their story in truth: they were youths who believed in their Lord, and We advanced them in guidance:

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Nahnu naqussu AAalayka nabaahum bialhaqqi innahum fityatun amanoo birabbihim wazidnahum hudan

14.arabic: وَرَبَطْنَا عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِمْ إِذْ قَامُوا۟ فَقَالُوا۟ رَبُّنَا رَبُّ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ لَن نَّدْعُوَا۟ مِن دُونِهِۦٓ إِلَـٰهًا ۖ لَّقَدْ قُلْنَآ إِذًا شَطَطًا

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And strengthened their hearts when they stood up and said: "Our Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth. We shall invoke no god beside Him, for we shall have uttered a blasphemy then.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And We made their hearts steadfast when they stood up and said, “Our Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth – we shall not worship any other deity except Him – if it were, we have then said something excessive.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And We strengthened their hearts, when they stood up and said, 'Our Lord is the Lord of the heavens and earth; we will not call upon any god, apart from Him, or then we had spoken outrage.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And We braced their hearts when they stood forth and said: our Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth; never we shall call upon a god beside him; for then we shall be saying an abomination.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And We made their hearts firm and strong (with the light of Faith in Allah and bestowed upon them patience to bear the separation of their kith and kin and dwellings, etc.) when they stood up and said: "Our Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, never shall we call upon any ilah (god) other than Him; if we did, we should indeed have uttered an enormity in disbelief.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And We strengthened their hearts, when they stood up and said, “Our Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth; we will not call on any god besides Him, for then we would have spoken an outrage.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: and strengthened their hearts when they stood up and proclaimed: "Our Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth. We shall call upon no other god beside Him; (for if we did so), we shall be uttering a blasphemy."

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And We made their hearts firm and strong when they stood up and said: "Our Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth, never shall we call upon any god other than Him; if we did, we should indeed have uttered an enormity in disbelief.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And We made firm their hearts when they stood forth and said: Our Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth. We cry unto no Allah beside Him, for then should we utter an enormity.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: and fortified their hearts, when they stood up and said, ‘Our Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth. We will never invoke any god besides Him, for then we shall certainly have said an atrocious lie.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: We strengthened their hearts when they stood up and said: 'Our Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth. We will call on no other god except Him; (for if we did), we would have spoken outrageously (in disbelief),

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And We made firm their hearts when they stood up and said, "Our Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth. Never will we invoke besides Him any deity. We would have certainly spoken, then, an excessive transgression.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We strengthened their hearts when they stood up against the idols and said, "Our Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth. We shall never worship anyone other than Him lest we commit blasphemy.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And We strengthened their hearts with patience, when they stood up and said: Our Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth; we will by no means call upon any god besides Him, for then indeed we should have said an extravagant thing.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: We strengthened their hearts, when they rose up and declared, "Our Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth. Never shall we call upon any deity other than Him: for that would be an outrageous thing to do.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: We gave strength to their hearts: Behold, they stood up and said: "Our Lord is the Lord of the heavens and of the earth: never shall we call upon any god other than Him: if we did, we should indeed have uttered an enormity!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Warabatna AAala quloobihim ith qamoo faqaloo rabbuna rabbu alssamawati waalardi lan nadAAuwa min doonihi ilahan laqad qulna ithan shatatan

15.arabic: هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ قَوْمُنَا ٱتَّخَذُوا۟ مِن دُونِهِۦٓ ءَالِهَةً ۖ لَّوْلَا يَأْتُونَ عَلَيْهِم بِسُلْطَـٰنٍۭ بَيِّنٍ ۖ فَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّنِ ٱفْتَرَىٰ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ كَذِبًا

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: As for these our people here, they have taken to other gods apart from Him. Then why do they not bring a clear authority for them? Who is more wicked than he who fabricates a lie against God?"

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “These – the people of ours – have set up Gods besides Allah; why do they not bring a clear proof regarding them? And who is more unjust than one who fabricates a lie against Allah?”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: These our people have taken to them other gods, apart from Him. Ah, if only they would bring some clear authority regarding them! But who does greater evil than he who forges against God a lie?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: These, our people, have taken for themselves gods beside Him - wherefore bring they not for them an authority manifest? --and who doth greater wrong than he who fabricateth against God a lie?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "These our people have taken for worship aliha (gods) other than Him (Allah). Why do they not bring for them a clear authority? And who does more wrong than he who invents a lie against Allah.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: “These people, our people, have taken to themselves gods other than Him. Why do they not bring a clear proof concerning them? Who, then, does greater wrong than he who invents lies and attributes them to God?”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: (Then they conferred among themselves and said): "These men, our own people, have taken others as gods beside Him: why do they not bring any clear evidence that they indeed are gods? Who can be more unjust than he who foists a lie on Allah?

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: These our people have taken for worship gods other than Him. Why do they not bring for them a clear authority And who does more wrong than he who invents a lie against Allah.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: These, our people, have chosen (other) gods beside Him though they bring no clear warrant (vouchsafed) to them. And who doth greater wrong than he who inventeth a lie concerning Allah?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: These—our people—have taken gods besides Him. Why do they not bring any clear authority touching them? So who is a greater wrongdoer than he who fabricates a lie against Allah?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: These, our nation have taken to themselves gods, other than Allah. Why do they not bring some clear authority regarding them! Who does greater evil than he who forges a lie against Allah'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: These, our people, have taken besides Him deities. Why do they not bring for [worship of] them a clear authority? And who is more unjust than one who invents about Allah a lie?"

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Our people have considered other things equal to God. Why cannot they present clear proof in support of their claim. Who is more unjust than one who invents falsehood against God?"

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: These our people have taken gods besides Him; why do they not produce any clear authority in their support? Who is then more unjust than he who forges a lie against Allah?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: These people of ours have taken deities other than Him. Why do they not produce clear evidence about them? Who is more wicked than the man who invents a falsehood against God?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "These our people have taken for worship gods other than Him: why do they not bring forward an authority clear (and convincing) for what they do? Who doth more wrong than such as invent a falsehood against Allah?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Haolai qawmuna ittakhathoo min doonihi alihatan lawla yatoona AAalayhim bisultanin bayyinin faman athlamu mimmani iftara AAala Allahi kathiban

16.arabic: وَإِذِ ٱعْتَزَلْتُمُوهُمْ وَمَا يَعْبُدُونَ إِلَّا ٱللَّهَ فَأْوُۥٓا۟ إِلَى ٱلْكَهْفِ يَنشُرْ لَكُمْ رَبُّكُم مِّن رَّحْمَتِهِۦ وَيُهَيِّئْ لَكُم مِّنْ أَمْرِكُم مِّرْفَقًا

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: (And they said to each other:) "Now that you have withdrawn from them and what they worship beside God, it is better to take refuge in the cave. Your Lord may bestow of His mercy on you, and facilitate your affair."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “And when you have disassociated yourself from them and all what they worship besides Allah – so take refuge in the Cave – your Lord will spread His mercy for you and arrange ease for you in your affairs.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: So, when you have gone apart from them and that they serve, excepting God, take refuge in the Cave, and your Lord will unfold to you of His mercy, and will furnish -- you with a gentle issue of your affair.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And now when ye have withdrawn from them and that which they worship, except God, betake yourselves to the cave; your Lord will unfold for you some of His mercy, and will prepare for you of your affair an easy arrangement.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: (The young men said to one another): "And when you withdraw from them, and that which they worship, except Allah, then seek refuge in the Cave, your Lord will open a way for you from His Mercy and will make easy for you your affair (i.e. will give you what you will need of provision, dwelling, etc.)."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: “Now that you have withdrawn from them, and from what they worship besides God, take shelter in the cave. And your Lord will unfold His mercy for you, and will set your affair towards ease.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: And now that you have dissociated yourselves from them and from whatever they worship beside Allah, go and seek refuge in the Cave. Your Lord will extend His mercy to you and will provide for you the means for the disposal of your affairs."

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: (The young men said to one another:) "And when you withdraw from them, and that which they worship, except Allah, then seek refuge in the cave; your Lord will open a way for you from His mercy and will make easy for you your affair."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And when ye withdraw from them and that which they worship except Allah, then seek refuge in the Cave; your Lord will spread for you of His mercy and will prepare for you a pillow in your plight.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: When you have dissociated yourselves from them and from what they worship except Allah, then take refuge in the Cave. Your Lord will unfold His mercy for you, and He will help you on to ease in your affair.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: When you depart from them and from what they worship, other than Allah, seek refuge in the Cave. Allah will extend His Mercy to you and will furnish you with a gentle issue of your affair.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [The youths said to one another], "And when you have withdrawn from them and that which they worship other than Allah, retreat to the cave. Your Lord will spread out for you of His mercy and will prepare for you from your affair facility."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (They were told), "Now that you have abandoned them and what they worship instead of God, seek refuge in the cave. God will, certainly, grant you mercy and provide you with help to safely get out of this trouble."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And when you forsake them and what they worship save Allah, betake yourselves for refuge to the cave; your Lord will extend to you largely of His mercy and provide for you a profitable course in your affair.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: "Now that you have withdrawn from them and from all that they worship instead of God, take refuge in that cave; your Lord will extend His mercy to you and will make fitting provision for you in your situation."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "When ye turn away from them and the things they worship other than Allah, betake yourselves to the Cave: Your Lord will shower His mercies on you and disposes of your affair towards comfort and ease."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waithi iAAtazaltumoohum wama yaAAbudoona illa Allaha fawoo ila alkahfi yanshur lakum rabbukum min rahmatihi wayuhayyi lakum min amrikum mirfaqan

17.arabic: وَتَرَى ٱلشَّمْسَ إِذَا طَلَعَت تَّزَٰوَرُ عَن كَهْفِهِمْ ذَاتَ ٱلْيَمِينِ وَإِذَا غَرَبَت تَّقْرِضُهُمْ ذَاتَ ٱلشِّمَالِ وَهُمْ فِى فَجْوَةٍ مِّنْهُ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ مِنْ ءَايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ ۗ مَن يَهْدِ ٱللَّهُ فَهُوَ ٱلْمُهْتَدِ ۖ وَمَن يُضْلِلْ فَلَن تَجِدَ لَهُۥ وَلِيًّا مُّرْشِدًا

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: You may well have seen when the sun came up it moved away to the right of the cave, and when it went down it turned away to the left, and they stayed in its open space. This was among the signs of God. Whoever is guided by God follows the right path; and whosoever goes astray will not find a guide to show him the way.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And O dear Prophet (Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him) you will see the sun that when it rises it shifts away to the right of their cave, and when it sets it shifts away to their left, and they are in the open ground of that cave; this is from among the signs of Allah; whomever Allah guides – only he is therefore guided; and whomever He sends astray – you will never find for him a friend who guides.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And thou mightest have seen the sun, when it rose, inclining from their Cave towards the right, and, when it set, passing them by on the left, while they were in a broad fissure of the Cave. That was one of God's signs; whomsoever God guides, he is rightly guided, and whomsoever He leads astray, thou wilt not find for him a protector to direct.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And thou Woudst behold the sun when it riseth veering away from their cave on the right, and when it setteth, passing them by on the left, while they were in the spacious part thereof: that is of the signs of Allah. Whomsoever Allah guideth, he indeed is the guided; and whomsoever He sendeth astray, for him thou wilt never find a directing friend.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And you might have seen the sun, when it rose, declining to the right from their Cave, and when it set, turning away from them to the left, while they lay in the midst of the Cave. That is (one) of the Ayat (proofs, evidences, signs) of Allah. He whom Allah guides, is rightly guided; but he whom He sends astray, for him you will find no Wali (guiding friend) to lead him (to the right Path).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: You would have seen the sun, when it rose, veering away from their cave towards the right, and when it sets, moving away from them to the left, as they lay in the midst of the cave. That was one of God’s wonders. He whom God guides is truly guided; but he whom He misguides, for him you will find no directing friend.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Had you seen them in the Cave it would have appeared to you that when the sun rose, it moved away from their Cave to the right; and when it set, it turned away from them to the left, while they remained in a spacious hollow in the Cave. This is one of the Signs of Allah. Whomsoever Allah guides, he alone is led aright; and whomsoever Allah lets go astray, you will find for him no guardian to direct him.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And you might have seen the sun, when it rose, declining to the right from their cave, and when it set, turning away from them to the left, while they lay in the midst of the cave. That is from the Ayat of Allah. He whom Allah guides, he is the rightly-guided; but he whom He sends astray, for him you will find no guardian to lead him.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And thou mightest have seen the sun when it rose move away from their cave to the right, and when it set go past them on the left, and they were in the cleft thereof. That was (one) of the portents of Allah. He whom Allah guideth, he indeed is led aright, and he whom He sendeth astray, for him thou wilt not find a guiding friend.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: You may see the sun, when it rises, slanting toward the right of their cave, and, when it sets, cut across them towards the left, and they are in a cavern within it. That is one of Allah’s signs. Whomever Allah guides is rightly guided, and whomever He leads astray, you will never find for him any guardian or guide.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: You might have seen the rising sun incline towards the right of their Cave, and, as it set go past them on the left, while they stayed within an open space in the Cave. That was one of the signs of Allah. He whom Allah guides is rightly guided; but he whom He leads astray you shall not find for him a guardian to guide him.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And [had you been present], you would see the sun when it rose, inclining away from their cave on the right, and when it set, passing away from them on the left, while they were [laying] within an open space thereof. That was from the signs of Allah. He whom Allah guides is the [rightly] guided, but he whom He leaves astray - never will you find for him a protecting guide.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: No sunlight could reach them during their sleep in the cave. One could see the rising sun decline to the right of their cave and the setting sun move its way to the left whilst they were sleeping in an opening of the cave. This is one of the miracles of God. Whomever God guides receives the right guidance and you will never find a guardian or guide for those whom He causes to go astray.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And you might see the sun when it rose, decline from their cave towards the right hand, and when it set, leave them behind on the left while they were in a wide space thereof. This is of the signs of Allah; whomsoever Allah guides, he is the rightly guided one, and whomsoever He causes to err, you shall not find for him any friend to lead (him) aright.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: The sun could be observed to incline away from their cave on the right, as it rose, and to turn away from them on the left, when it set, while they lay in the wide space inside the cave. This is one of the signs of God. He whom God guides is rightly guided; but for him whom He lets go astray, you will find no helper or guide.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Thou wouldst have seen the sun, when it rose, declining to the right from their Cave, and when it set, turning away from them to the left, while they lay in the open space in the midst of the Cave. Such are among the Signs of Allah: He whom Allah, guides is rightly guided; but he whom Allah leaves to stray,- for him wilt thou find no protector to lead him to the Right Way.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Watara alshshamsa itha talaAAat tazawaru AAan kahfihim thata alyameeni waitha gharabat taqriduhum thata alshshimali wahum fee fajwatin minhu thalika min ayati Allahi man yahdi Allahu fahuwa almuhtadi waman yudlil falan tajida lahu waliyyan murshidan

18.arabic: وَتَحْسَبُهُمْ أَيْقَاظًا وَهُمْ رُقُودٌ ۚ وَنُقَلِّبُهُمْ ذَاتَ ٱلْيَمِينِ وَذَاتَ ٱلشِّمَالِ ۖ وَكَلْبُهُم بَـٰسِطٌ ذِرَاعَيْهِ بِٱلْوَصِيدِ ۚ لَوِ ٱطَّلَعْتَ عَلَيْهِمْ لَوَلَّيْتَ مِنْهُمْ فِرَارًا وَلَمُلِئْتَ مِنْهُمْ رُعْبًا

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: You may have thought that they were awake, yet they were asleep. We made them turn right and left, while their dog lay with his forelegs stretched across the threshold. If you had looked at them you would have surely turned away and fled with horror at the sight.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And you may think they are awake, whereas they are asleep; and We turn them over to the right and the left – and their dog is on the threshold of the cave, with its paws outstretched; O listener, were you to look at them closely, you would turn back running away from them, and be filled with their dread.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Thou wouldst have thought them awake, as they lay sleeping, while We turned them now to the right, now to the left,' and their dog 'stretching its paws on the threshold. Hadst thou observed them surely thou wouldst have turned thy back on them in flight, and been filled with terror of them.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And thou wouldst have deemed them awakes whereas they were asleep and We turned them over on the right side and on the left side, while their dog stretched forth his two forelegs on the threshold. If thou hadst observed them, thou wouldst have surely turned away from them in fright, and wouldst have surely been filled with awe of them.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And you would have thought them awake, while they were asleep. And We turned them on their right and on their left sides, and their dog stretching forth his two forelegs at the entrance [of the Cave or in the space near to the entrance of the Cave (as a guard at the gate)]. Had you looked at them, you would certainly have turned back from them in flight, and would certainly have been filled with awe of them.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: You would think them awake, although they were asleep. And We turned them over to the right, and to the left, with their dog stretching its paws across the threshold. Had you looked at them, you would have turned away from them in flight, and been filled with fear of them.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: On seeing them you would fancy them to be awake though they were asleep; and We caused them to turn their sides to their right and to their left, and their dog sat stretching out its forelegs on the threshold of the Cave. Had you looked upon them you would have certainly fled away from them, their sight filling you with terror.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And you would have thought them awake, whereas they were asleep. And We turned them on their right and on their left sides, and their dog stretching forth his two forelegs at the Wasid. Had you happened upon them, you would certainly have turned back from them in flight, and would certainly have been filled with awe of them.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And thou wouldst have deemed them waking though they were asleep, and We caused them to turn over to the right and the left, and their dog stretching out his paws on the threshold. If thou hadst observed them closely thou hadst assuredly turned away from them in flight, and hadst been filled with awe of them.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: You will suppose them to be awake, although they are asleep. We turn them to the right and to the left, and their dog [lies] stretching its forelegs at the threshold. If you come upon them, you will surely turn to flee from them, and you will surely be filled with a terror of them.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: You might have thought them awake, though they were sleeping. We turned them about to the right and to the left, while their dog stretched its paws at the entrance. Had you seen them you would surely have become filled with terror and turned your back on them in flight.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And you would think them awake, while they were asleep. And We turned them to the right and to the left, while their dog stretched his forelegs at the entrance. If you had looked at them, you would have turned from them in flight and been filled by them with terror.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: One would think them (the youths) awake while, in fact, they were sleeping. We turned their bodies from right to left and their dog stretched its front legs on the ground. Had one looked them over, he would have run away from them in terror.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And you might think them awake while they were asleep and We turned them about to the right and to the left, while their dog (lay) outstretching its paws at the entrance; if you looked at them you would certainly turn back from them in flight, and you would certainly be filled with awe because of them.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: You would have thought they were awake, though they lay asleep. We turned them over, to the right and the left, while their dog lay at the cave's entrance with legs outstretched. Had you looked down and seen them, you would have surely turned your back and fled in terror.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Thou wouldst have deemed them awake, whilst they were asleep, and We turned them on their right and on their left sides: their dog stretching forth his two fore-legs on the threshold: if thou hadst come up on to them, thou wouldst have certainly turned back from them in flight, and wouldst certainly have been filled with terror of them.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Watahsabuhum ayqathan wahum ruqoodun wanuqallibuhum thata alyameeni wathata alshshimali wakalbuhum basitun thiraAAayhi bialwaseedi lawi ittalaAAta AAalayhim lawallayta minhum firaran walamulita minhum ruAAban

19.arabic: وَكَذَٰلِكَ بَعَثْنَـٰهُمْ لِيَتَسَآءَلُوا۟ بَيْنَهُمْ ۚ قَالَ قَآئِلٌ مِّنْهُمْ كَمْ لَبِثْتُمْ ۖ قَالُوا۟ لَبِثْنَا يَوْمًا أَوْ بَعْضَ يَوْمٍ ۚ قَالُوا۟ رَبُّكُمْ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا لَبِثْتُمْ فَٱبْعَثُوٓا۟ أَحَدَكُم بِوَرِقِكُمْ هَـٰذِهِۦٓ إِلَى ٱلْمَدِينَةِ فَلْيَنظُرْ أَيُّهَآ أَزْكَىٰ طَعَامًا فَلْيَأْتِكُم بِرِزْقٍ مِّنْهُ وَلْيَتَلَطَّفْ وَلَا يُشْعِرَنَّ بِكُمْ أَحَدًا

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Even so We roused them that they may question one another. One of them asked: "How long have you stayed here?" They said: "A day or less than a day." "Your Lord knows best," they said, "how long you have stayed. So send one of you to the town with this money of yours to look for wholesome food and bring it for you; but he should be careful not to let your presence known.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And similarly We awakened them so that they may enquire about each other; a speaker among them said, “How long have you stayed here?” Some among them said, “We have stayed a day or part of a day”; the others said, “Your Lord well knows how long you have stayed; therefore send one of you to the city with this silver coin – he may then check which food available there is purer, in order to bring some of it for you to eat – and he must be courteous and not inform anyone about you.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And even so We raised them up again that they might question one another. One of them said, 'How long have you tarried?' They said, 'We have tarried a day, or part of a day.' They said, 'Your Lord knows very well how long you have tarried. Now send one of you forth with this silver to the city, and let him look for which of them has purest food, and bring you provision thereof; let him be courteous, and apprise no man of you.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And likewise we raised them up that they might question among themselves; there spake a speaker from amongst them: how long have ye tarried? They said we have tarried a day or part of a day. They said, your Lord knoweth best how long ye have tarried; now send one of you with this your money unto the city and let him see which food is purest there, and let him bring you a provision thereof, and let him be circumspect, and let him by no means discover you to anycne.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Likewise, We awakened them (from their long deep sleep) that they might question one another. A speaker from among them said: "How long have you stayed (here)?" They said: "We have stayed (perhaps) a day or part of a day." They said: "Your Lord (Alone) knows best how long you have stayed (here). So send one of you with this silver coin of yours to the town, and let him find out which is the good lawful food, and bring some of that to you. And let him be careful and let no man know of you.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Even so, We awakened them, so that they may ask one another. A speaker among them said, “How long have you stayed?” They said, “We have stayed a day, or part of a day.” They said, “Your Lord knows best how long you have stayed.” “Send one of you to the city, with this money of yours, and let him see which food is most suitable, and let him bring you some provision thereof. And let him be gentle, and let no one become aware of you.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Likewise, We roused them in a miraculous way that they might question one another. One of them asked: "How long did you remain (in this state)?" The others said: "We remained so for a day or part of a day." Then they said: "Your Lord knows better how long we remained in this state. Now send one of you to the city with this coin of yours and let him see who has the best food, and let him buy some provisions from there. Let him be cautious and not inform anyone of our whereabouts.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Likewise, We woke them that they might question one another. A speaker among them said: "How long have you stayed (here)" They said: "We have stayed a day or part of a day." They said: "Your Lord knows best how long you have stayed (here). So send one of you with this silver coin of yours to the town, and let him find out which is the Azka food, and bring some of that to you. And let him be careful and let no man know of you."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And in like manner We awakened them that they might question one another. A speaker from among them said: How long have ye tarried? They said: We have tarried a day or some part of a day, (Others) said: Your Lord best knoweth what ye have tarried. Now send one of you with this your silver coin unto the city, and let him see what food is purest there and bring you a supply thereof. Let him be courteous and let no man know of you.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: So it was that We aroused them [from sleep] so that they might question one another. One of them said, ‘How long have you stayed [here]?’ They said, ‘We have stayed a day, or part of a day.’ They said, ‘Your Lord knows best how long you have stayed. Send one of you to the city with this money. Let him observe which of them has the purest food, and bring you provisions from there. Let him be attentive, and let him not make anyone aware of you.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: As such We revived them so that they might question one another. 'How long have you stayed here' asked one of them. 'We have been here a day, or part of it' they replied. They said: 'Your Lord knows best how long we have stayed here. Let one of you go to the city with this silver (coin) and let him search for one who has the purest food and bring provision from it. Let him be courteous, but let no one sense it is you.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And similarly, We awakened them that they might question one another. Said a speaker from among them, "How long have you remained [here]?" They said, "We have remained a day or part of a day." They said, "Your Lord is most knowing of how long you remained. So send one of you with this silver coin of yours to the city and let him look to which is the best of food and bring you provision from it and let him be cautious. And let no one be aware of you.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: We roused them from their sleep so that they would question each other about their stay in the cave. One of them said, "How long do you think we have stayed here?" They replied, "A day or part of a day." They added, " Your Lord knows better how long we have stayed here. Let us send one of us with this money to the city to get some pure food so that we might eat. He should be careful so that no one will know about us. If they were to recognize us,

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And thus did We rouse them that they might question each other. A speaker among them said: How long have you tarried? They said: We have tarried for a day or a part of a day. (Others) said: Your Lord knows best how long you have tarried. Now send one of you with this silver (coin) of yours to the city, then let him see which of them has purest food, so let him bring you provision from it, and let him behave with gentleness, and by no means make your case known to any one:

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: In the course of time, We raised them up again so that they might question one another. One of them asked, "How long have you stayed [here]?" They said, "We have stayed a day, or part of a day." But others said, "Your Lord knows best how long you have stayed here. Let one of you go then with these silver coins to the town, and let him find out what food is purest there, and bring you back a supply of it. Let him conduct himself with caution and not disclose your whereabouts to anyone:

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Such (being their state), we raised them up (from sleep), that they might question each other. Said one of them, "How long have ye stayed (here)?" They said, "We have stayed (perhaps) a day, or part of a day." (At length) they (all) said, "Allah (alone) knows best how long ye have stayed here.... Now send ye then one of you with this money of yours to the town: let him find out which is the best food (to be had) and bring some to you, that (ye may) satisfy your hunger therewith: And let him behave with care and courtesy, and let him not inform any one about you.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wakathalika baAAathnahum liyatasaaloo baynahum qala qailun minhum kam labithtum qaloo labithna yawman aw baAAda yawmin qaloo rabbukum aAAlamu bima labithtum faibAAathoo ahadakum biwariqikum hathihi ila almadeenati falyanthur ayyuha azka taAAaman falyatikum birizqin minhu walyatalattaf wala yushAAiranna bikum ahadan

20.arabic: إِنَّهُمْ إِن يَظْهَرُوا۟ عَلَيْكُمْ يَرْجُمُوكُمْ أَوْ يُعِيدُوكُمْ فِى مِلَّتِهِمْ وَلَن تُفْلِحُوٓا۟ إِذًا أَبَدًا

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: If they come to know of you they will stone you to death, or force you to go back to their creed; then you will never succeed."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “Indeed if they come to know about you, they will stone you or turn you back to their religion – and if so, you will never prosper.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: If they should get knowledge of you they will stone you, or restore you to their creed, then you will not prosper ever.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Verily they, if they come to know of you, would stone you or make you revert to their faith, and lo! then ye will never fare well.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "For if they come to know of you, they will stone you (to death or abuse and harm you) or turn you back to their religion, and in that case you will never be successful."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: “If they discover you, they will stone you, or force you back into their religion; then you will never be saved.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: For if they should come upon us, they will stone us to death or force us to revert to their faith whereafter we shall never prosper."

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "For, if they come to know of you, they will stone you or turn you back to their religion; and in that case you will never be successful."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: For they, if they should come to know of you, will stone you or turn you back to their religion; then ye will never prosper.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Indeed should they prevail over you, they will [either] stone you [to death], or force you back into their creed, and then you will never be saved. ’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: For, if they appear in front of you, they will stone you to death or restore you to their religion. Then you will never prosper'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Indeed, if they come to know of you, they will stone you or return you to their religion. And never would you succeed, then - ever."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: they would certainly stone us to death or force us to follow their religion. Then we shall never be able to have everlasting happiness."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: For surely if they prevail against you they would stone you to death or force you back to their religion, and then you will never succeed.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: for if they find you out, they will stone you to death, or force you back into their faith. In that case you would never prosper."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "For if they should come upon you, they would stone you or force you to return to their cult, and in that case ye would never attain prosperity."

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Innahum in yathharoo AAalaykum yarjumookum aw yuAAeedookum fee millatihim walan tuflihoo ithan abadan

21.arabic: وَكَذَٰلِكَ أَعْثَرْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ لِيَعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّ وَعْدَ ٱللَّهِ حَقٌّ وَأَنَّ ٱلسَّاعَةَ لَا رَيْبَ فِيهَآ إِذْ يَتَنَـٰزَعُونَ بَيْنَهُمْ أَمْرَهُمْ ۖ فَقَالُوا۟ ٱبْنُوا۟ عَلَيْهِم بُنْيَـٰنًا ۖ رَّبُّهُمْ أَعْلَمُ بِهِمْ ۚ قَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ غَلَبُوا۟ عَلَىٰٓ أَمْرِهِمْ لَنَتَّخِذَنَّ عَلَيْهِم مَّسْجِدًا

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Thus did We inform the people about them that they may know the promise of God is true, and there is no doubt that the Hour will come. As they were arguing among themselves as to what should be done with them, (some) said: "Erect a monument over them. Their Lord is best cognisant of them." Those who prevailed, said: "We shall build a place of worship over their (sepulchre)."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And this is how We made them known for people to know that the promise of Allah is true and that there is no doubt concerning the Last Day; when the people began disputing among themselves regarding them, they said, “Construct a building over their cave”; their Lord well knows them; those who dominated in this matter said, “We promise we will build a mosque over them.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And even so We made them stumble upon them, that they might know that God's promise is true, and that the Hour -- there is no doubt of it. When they were contending among themselves of their affair then they said, 'Build over them a building; their Lord knows of them very well.' Said those who prevailed over their affair, 'We will raise over them a place of worship.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And likewise We caused their affair to be lit upon that they might know that the promise of Allah is true, and that the Hour- there is no doubt thereof, Recall what time they were disputing among themselves regarding their affair, and then they said: build over them a building--their Lord is the Best Knower about them-then those who prevailed in their affair said: surely we will raise over them a place of worship.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And thus We made their case known to the people, that they might know that the Promise of Allah is true, and that there can be no doubt about the Hour. (Remember) when they (the people of the city) disputed among themselves about their case, they said: "Construct a building over them, their Lord knows best about them," (then) those who won their point said (most probably the disbelievers): "We verily shall build a place of worship over them."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: So it was, that We caused them to be discovered, that they would know that the promise of God is true, and that of the Hour there is no doubt. As they were disputing their case among themselves, they said, “Build over them a building.” Their Lord knows best about them. Those who prevailed over their case said, “We will set up over them a place of worship.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Thus did We make their case known to the people of the city so that they might know that Allah's promise is true, and that there is absolutely no doubt that the Hour will come to pass. But instead of giving thought to this, they disputed with one another concerning the People of the Cave, some saying: "Build a wall over them. Their Lord alone knows best about them." But those who prevailed over their affairs said: "We shall build a place of worship over them."

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And thus We made their case known, that they might know that the promise of Allah is true, and that there can be no doubt about the Hour. (Remember) when they (the people) disputed among themselves about their case, they said: "Construct a building over them; their Lord knows best about them," (then) those who won their point said: "We verily, shall build a place of worship over them."

translations.author: Pickthall
